analysis.summary: The United States and Israel launched joint military strikes against Iran beginning February 28, 2026, initiating a conflict now in its fourth week. The war has escalated to include mutual strikes on nuclear facilities, Iranian missile attacks on Israel and a joint UK-US base at Diego Garcia, and a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, with President Trump threatening to 'obliterate' Iranian power plants if the strait is not fully reopened within 48 hours. Iran has responded by declaring the strait open only to non-enemy-linked ships and firing long-range missiles, while international actors including Saudi Arabia, Japan, and European nations are drawn into the crisis.
